title: The Value of Proper Intelligence to Any Successful Military Campaign is Not to Be Underestimated

{{Quote|In the name of the Emperor, I claim this planet as Imperial territory. Surrender and no harm will come to you.|Imperial officer, about to be killed|<ref name="The Value of Proper Intelligence to Any Successful Military Campaign is Not to Be Underestimated">{{Tales|19|The Value of Proper Intelligence to Any Successful Military Campaign is Not to Be Underestimated}}</ref>}}
"'''The Value of Proper Intelligence to Any Successful Military Campaign is Not to Be Underestimated'''" is a two-page [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] that appeared in ''[[Star Wars Tales 19]]'' and was later collected in ''[[Star Wars Tales Volume 5]]''. It was written by [[Ken Lizzl]], penciled by [[Lucas Marangon]], and lettered by [[Steve Dutro]]. The comic tells the story of a small [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] detachment [[Mission to Gibbela|sent to conquer]] the rural [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Gibbela]]. Although they expected to meet little resistance, the Imperials were wiped out by a [[Unidentified Dazouri farmer|polymorphic native]].

==Plot summary==
On the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Gibbela]], an [[Agriworld/Legends|agricultural world]] well off any [[Hyperspace route/Legends|trade routes]], a tiny [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Dazouri|native]] [[Unidentified Dazouri farmer|farmer]] is [[Farm/Legends|farming]] on a plot of land. A small [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] contingent lands on the farm in a [[Sentinel-class landing craft/Legends|''Sentinel''-class landing craft]], planning on [[Mission to Gibbela|taking over]] the planet. Several [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] and an [[Unidentified Imperial officer (Gibbela)|officer]] disembark the [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] and demand that the farmer surrender. However, much to the Imperials' surprise, the farmer transforms into a gigantic clawed beast and [[Death/Legends|kills]] all of them. The creature shrinks back to its original size and continues its fieldwork, using the remaining [[Stormtrooper armor/Legends|armor]] and weapons of the deceased Imperials as farm equipment. In the future, [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] avoid Gibbela because of its deadly inhabitants.

==Development==
[[File:Gibbela.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Gibbela native uses the captured Imperial gear as farming equipment.]]
A short, two-page [[Star Wars comic books|comic]], "The Value of Proper Intelligence to Any Successful Military Campaign is Not to Be Underestimated" first appeared in [[Dark Horse Comics]]' ''[[Star Wars Tales 19]]'', published on [[May 14]], [[2004]]. It was later collected in ''[[Star Wars Tales Volume 5]]'', which was released on [[February 23]], [[2005]]. The comic was written by [[Ken Lizzl]], penciled by [[Lucas Marangon]], and lettered by [[Steve Dutro]].

==Continuity==
Originally, the [[canon]]icity of "The Value of Proper Intelligence to Any Successful Military Campaign is Not to Be Underestimated" was considered ambiguous, or S-canon, because the comic had been published in an issue of ''Star Wars Tales'' before [[Star Wars Tales 21|issue 21]].<ref name="Leland Chee">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=752#14952461|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-06-04|time=3:05 AM}}</ref> However, ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', released in [[December]] [[2008]], referenced the events of the comic in its entry on Gibbela, promoting the comic to C-canon, or Continuity Canon, which consists of all published works recognized as part of the official continuity.<ref name="LCG">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?forumID=24&start=15&threadID=152583&msRange=15#5706120|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2004-01-21|time=9:16 AM}}</ref>
